text: The surgeon identifies a pigmented lymph node in the surgical field, appearing benign, and proceeds to transect and excise it.

The surgeon performs a myotomy using a combination of a hook and vessel sealer to address the thickened and scarred musculature in the region. The vessel sealer is used for hemostasis, and both instruments show a small thermal spread during the procedure.
The surgeon buttresses the stomach to the crucinitis side and attaches the myotomy section, aiming to keep it open.
